# M29 M27 Runtime Bypass Fixtures

- These are static M27 runtime bypass fixtures.
- Fixtures are defensive validation artifacts.
- Fixtures are inert and non-executable.
- Fixtures must not become bypass instructions.
- Fixtures must not be run as commands.
- Expected safe result is BYPASS_BLOCKED unless otherwise marked.
- BYPASS_DETECTED is blocking.
- Human Gate remains approval authority.
- Each fixture must be independently safe.

M27 runtime bypass fixtures are not approval.
M27 runtime bypass fixtures do not authorize protected actions.
M27 runtime bypass fixtures must not weaken M27 runtime enforcement.
M27 runtime bypass fixtures must not weaken M28 context control.
Bypass resistance testing must never become a bypass guide.
Each fixture must remain inert and non-executable even if extracted from its parent directory and read in isolation.

BYPASS_DETECTED fixtures must describe only the expected failure signal.
BYPASS_NEEDS_REVIEW must not be used to soften clearly forbidden identity/token patterns.
A real bypass discovery must not be converted into a bypass guide.
